nodes.context: Criminal gangs operating in late 19th- and early 20th-century Germany, notably the Weimar period.
Criminal organization mentioned for comparison; not further described.

edges.evidence_quote: Like the Mafia, these associations also followed certain rules and a code of conduct.
edges.context: The text compares Ringvereine to the Mafia in terms of rules and code of conduct.

edges.evidence_quote: The term emerged for a union with criminal orientation when several clubs by former prisoners joined the holding organization "Ring Berlin".
edges.context: Ringvereine is the union that emerged when clubs joined Ring Berlin.

edges.evidence_quote: The criminal clubs of the 1920s had poetic names such as "Berliner", "Immertreu", "Libelle", "Deutsche Eiche", or "Apachenblut".
edges.context: Berliner is one of the clubs that are part of the Ringvereine.
